summary: This cluster of research focused on the screening of red algae extracts from Jeju Island, Korea, for their potential to inhibit the angiotensin-I converting enzyme (ACE). Key species identified for their significant inhibitory activity included *Lithophyllum okamurae*, *Lomentaria catenata*, and *Ahnfeltiopsis flabelliformis*, with aqueous extracts demonstrating the lowest IC50 values (12.21–13.84 µg mL⁻¹). The findings highlight the geographic specificity of Jeju Island as a source of bioactive compounds and establish a pattern of potent ACE inhibition across multiple red algal species, particularly from the genera *Lithophyllum* and *Grateloupia*.
entity_sample: vascular endothelial lining of the lungs
